alignments.preprint_claim_text: HydrAMP is a conditional variational autoencoder that learns lower-dimensional, continuous representation of peptides and captures their antimicrobial properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HydrAMP is a conditional variational autoencoder that learns lower-dimensional, continuous representation of peptides and captures their antimicrobial properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: HydrAMP outperforms other approaches in diverse tasks, including unconstrained and analogue generation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Wet-lab experiments on five bacterial strains confirm high activity of nine peptides generated as analogues of clinically relevant prototypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Wet-lab experiments on five bacterial strains confirm high activity of nine peptides generated as analogues of clinically relevant prototypes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HydrAMP enables generation of diverse and potent peptides, making a step towards resolving the antimicrobial resistance crisis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HydrAMP enables generation of diverse and potent peptides, making a step towards resolving the antimicrobial resistance crisis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Better metacognitive performance is observed for committed decisions compared to observed decisions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a dissociation in the anterior prefrontal cortex and anterior insula related to confidence in decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Metacognitive performance improves when evidence accumulation is constrained upon committing a decision. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study proposes a computational model of confidence in which metacognitive performance improves when evidence accumulation is constrained upon committing a decision. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Latent variable models with nonlinear dependencies can capture beyond-pairwise constraints in biomolecules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSequence predicts the effects of mutations across deep mutational scanning experiments significantly better than site independent or pairwise models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSequence predicts the effects of mutations across various deep mutational scanning experiments substantially better than existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is learned in an unsupervised manner solely from sequence information and is grounded with biologically motivated priors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model is learned in an unsupervised manner solely on the basis of sequence information and is grounded with biologically motivated priors. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSequence reveals latent organization of sequence families and can be used to extrapolate to new parts of sequence space. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSequence reveals the latent organization of sequence families and can be used to explore new parts of sequence space. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: scAlign is an unsupervised deep learning method for data integration that can incorporate partial, overlapping, or a complete set of cell labels. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: scAlign's performance is state-of-the-art and robust to cross-dataset variation in cell type-specific expression and cell type composition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: scAlign's performance is state-of-the-art and robust to cross-dataset variation in cell type-specific expression and cell type composition. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework of scAlign is widely applicable to integration challenges in other domains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoBA is an autonomous AI agent designed for fully automated multi-omic analyses based on large language models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoBA simplifies the analytical process by requiring minimal user input while delivering detailed step-by-step plans for various bioinformatics tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoBA simplifies the analytical process by requiring minimal user input while delivering detailed step-by-step plans for various bioinformatics tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoBA offers multiple LLM backends, with options for both online and local usage, prioritizing data security and user privacy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoBA has adaptability in sync with emerging bioinformatics tools, differing from predefined pipelines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoBA has adaptability in sync with emerging bioinformatics tools, differing from predefined pipelines. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human body size delineates a distinct boundary on affordances, dividing objects into two discrete categories with each affording distinct action sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Body size delineates a distinct boundary on affordances, dividing objects into two discrete categories with each affording distinct action sets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The boundary of affordance perception shifts with imagined body sizes, suggesting a causal link between body size and affordance perception. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The boundary of affordance perception shifts with imagined body sizes, suggesting a causal link between body size and affordance perception. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ChatGPT exhibited a modest yet comparable affordance boundary at the scale of human body size, indicating that the boundary is not exclusively derived from organism-environment interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ChatGPT exhibited a modest yet comparable affordance boundary at the scale of human body size, suggesting the boundary is not exclusively derived from organism-environment interactions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study advocates a novel definition of object-ness in an affordance-based context, emphasizing the concept of embodied cognition in understanding intelligence constrained by physical attributes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study advocates a novel definition of object-ness in an affordance-based context, supporting the concept of embodied cognition in understanding intelligence constrained by physical attributes. (certainty=low, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple spike-timing-dependent plasticity (STDP) rules can lead to the stable encoding of memories in the absence of any control mechanism. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simple spike-timing-dependent plasticity (STDP) rules can lead to the stable encoding of memories in the absence of any control mechanism. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of two inhibitory STDP sub-populations is necessary for the formation of stable modules in the network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of two inhibitory STDP sub-populations is necessary for the formation of stable modules in the network. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibitory circuits promote long-term memory consolidation by inducing spontaneous memory recalls during the asynchronous irregular resting phase. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network settles into an asynchronous irregular resting-state after the learning phase, which is associated with spontaneous memory recalls fundamental for long-term consolidation of learned memories. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of inhibitory neurons controls the maximal memory capacity of the model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced model can serve as a test-bed for further investigations on the role played by STDP on memory storing and maintenance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional method predicts simple and complex cell responses to natural image stimuli as well as grating tuning curves. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple convolutional neural network method can estimate receptive field models for both simple and complex visual cortex cells from their responses to natural images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fitted models yield a continuum of values for the PReLU parameter across the sampled neurons, showing that the simple/complex nature of cells can vary in a continuous manner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fitted models yield a continuum of values for the PReLU parameter across the sampled neurons, indicating that the simple/complex nature of cells can vary in a continuous manner. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Complex-like cells respond less reliably than simple-like cells, and accounting for this unreliability improves predictive performance for complex cells. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Complex-like cells respond less reliably than simple-like cells, but compensation for this unreliability reveals predictive performance for complex cells that is proportionately closer to that for simple cells. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Almost all complex-like cells exhibit a lower response reliability to repeated presentations of the same stimuli compared to simple-like cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Complex-like cells respond less reliably than simple-like cells, but compensation for this unreliability reveals predictive performance for complex cells that is proportionately closer to that for simple cells. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model estimation approach reveals a diversity of Gabor-like spatial receptive field shapes, which lie along a continuum of spatial bandwidths. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retro-cues facilitate subsequent recall by transforming prioritised memory items into a prospective, action-oriented format. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: In the macaque lateral prefrontal cortex, prioritised items are rotated into a common subspace during the post-cue delay. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parallel geometry in the recurrent neural networks only develops when cued information is maintained in short-term memory for several cycles before readout. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parallel geometry in RNNs develops only when cued information is maintained in short-term memory for several cycles before readout. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study extends findings by analyzing the learning dynamics and connectivity patterns of recurrent neural networks, making predictions for future studies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Retro-cues facilitate subsequent recall by prioritising one item over another in short-term memory. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural responses in mouse and macaque primary visual cortex (V1) obey the predictions of the theory regarding the power law exponent of the eigenspectrum, with exponents of at least one. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural responses in mouse and macaque primary visual cortex (V1) obey the predictions of the theory regarding power law exponents of their eigenspectra. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Robust models have eigenspectra that decay slightly faster and have higher power law exponents than those of non-robust models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Robust models had preferred spatial frequency distributions more aligned with the measured spatial frequency distribution of macaque V1 cells. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dependence on high-frequency image features for image classification may be related to the image perturbations affecting models but not humans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It may be useful to penalize slow-decaying eigenspectra or to bias models to extract features of lower spatial frequencies during task-optimization to improve robustness and V1 neural response predictivity. (certainty=medium, type=suggestion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It may be useful to penalize slow-decaying eigenspectra or to bias models to extract features of lower spatial frequencies during task-optimization to improve robustness. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Robust models were quantitatively better models of V1 than non-robust models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model of goal-directed behaviour has a probabilistic interpretation and is centered on a recurrent spiking neural network representing the world model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The world model learns in parallel with its use for planning, and an arbitration mechanism decides when to exploit the world-model knowledge or to explore based on entropy-based confidence. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can learn to anticipate the consequences of actions in an autonomous fashion based on experience. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CELESTA framework significantly improves context classification and extracting contextual information in biomedical relation extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CELESTA framework significantly improves context classification performance in biomedical research. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework includes an out-of-distribution detector to distinguish between in-distribution and out-of-distribution instances within unlabeled data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The left anterior and middle temporal lobe (LATL; LMTL) are distinctively modulated by semantic composition and semantic association. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The left anterior temporal lobe (LATL) and left middle temporal lobe (LMTL) are distinctively modulated by semantic composition and semantic association. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LATL is mostly sensitive to high-association compositional phrases, while the LMTL responds more to low-association compositional phrases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LATL is mostly sensitive to high-association compositional phrases, while the LMTL responds more to low-association compositional phrases. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a continuous information flow from the anterior to the middle temporal region, suggesting that the integration of adjective and noun properties originates earlier in the LATL. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a continuous information flow from the anterior to the middle temporal region, suggesting that the integration of adjective and noun properties originates earlier in the LATL. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide insight into a functional dissociation within the left temporal lobe for compositional and distributional semantic processing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide insight into a functional dissociation within the left temporal lobe for compositional and distributional semantic processing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The fourth assemblage significantly positively correlated with three butyrate-producing functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces functional correlation results (butyrate-producing functions) not described in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NLP algorithm identified 507 cases of PCS with a positive predictive value of 82%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coded algorithm identified 1,142 patients with two or more PCS billing codes and had a positive predictive value of 76%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The coded algorithm identified 1142 patients with two or more PCS billing codes and had a PPV of 76%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both algorithms recovered known epidemiology of PCS, indicating that cases were more likely than controls to be female and to have pre-morbid diagnoses of anxiety, migraine, and PTSD. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Ascertainment based on coded data alone had a predictive value comparable to an NLP algorithm and maximized the number of included patients. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The NLP algorithm identified 507 cases of post-concussion syndrome (PCS) and 10,857 controls. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: EHRs are a valuable research tool for studying post-concussion syndrome (PCS). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method predicted 7 out of the 10 previously known cross-species interactions involved in pathogenesis between S. mansoni and its human host. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several predictions warranting experimental follow-up include interactions involving potential vaccine candidate antigens, protease inhibition, and immune evasion. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two novel putative interactions involving Schistosoma proteins, SmCE and Sm29, were predicted and experimentally characterized. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application framework provides an integrated methodology for the investigation of host-pathogen interactions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application framework provides an integrated methodology for investigation of host-pathogen interactions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution wording but with a lower certainty label, so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a protocol that generates testable predictions of S. mansoni-human protein interactions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary data suggest that elafin may be a novel substrate for SmCE. (certainty=low, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STAIG is a novel deep-learning model that integrates gene expression, spatial coordinates, and histological images using graph-contrastive learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: STAIG is a deep-learning model that integrates gene expression, spatial coordinates, and histological images using graph-contrastive learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: STAIG demonstrates the capability to recognize spatial regions with high precision and uncover new insights into tumor microenvironments. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ENzyme KInetics Estimator (ENKIE) predicts the value and uncertainty of KM and kcat parameters using Bayesian Multilevel Models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ENKIE achieves prediction performances comparable to deep learning approaches that utilize sequence and structure information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ENKIE achieves prediction performances comparable to deep learning approaches that utilize sequence and structure information. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models provide accurate uncertainty predictions and interpretable insights into the main sources of uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models provide calibrated uncertainty predictions and interpretable insights into the main sources of uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ENKIE is expected to simplify the construction of priors for Bayesian kinetic models of metabolism. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-tissue transcriptome mapping (MTM) framework enables the prediction of individualized expression profiles from any available tissue of an individual. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: MTM achieves superior sample-level and gene-level performance on unseen individuals by leveraging individualized cross-tissue information through multi-task learning. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MTM demonstrates high prediction accuracy and the ability to preserve individualized biological variations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MTM demonstrates high prediction accuracy and the ability to preserve individualized biological variations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CTRL: Cell Topography Reconstruction Learner is a label-free technique that uses Deep Learning and Fluorescence Exclusion to reconstruct cell topography and estimate mammalian cell volume from DIC microscopy images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieves quantitative accuracy and requires minimal sample preparation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieves quantitative accuracy and requires minimal sample preparation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using this method, we observe a noticeable reduction in cell size fluctuations during the cell cycle. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: In HT1080 fibrosarcoma cells, the cell size at division is positively correlated with the cell size at birth. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive performance of Illumina and Nanopore sequencing platforms are comparable, establishing Nanopore as a feasible alternative to Illumina sequencing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predictions from Illumina and Nanopore sequencing platforms are comparable, establishing Nanopore as a feasible alternative to Illumina sequencing. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a stabilized LASSO regression, the feature set was efficiently optimized from thousands to 40-60 Operational Taxonomic Units (OTUs), reducing prediction errors to less than half of what was obtained through full feature modeling. (certainty=high, type=result)

(certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continual improvements in Nanopore sequencing accuracy, along with optimized feature selection on a broader set of samples, provide a precise and cost-effective monitoring method for marine benthic environments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The features demonstrated strong predictive accuracy across sequencing technologies, with a high correlation between observed and predicted nEQR values. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Spastin is a microtubule-severing AAA+ ATPase that is crucial for axonal growth, branching, and regeneration. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutations in spastin impair its ability to sever microtubules effectively, leading to abnormal microtubule dynamics in neurons associated with hereditary spastic paraplegias (HSP). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The HSP-related R591S mutation causes the functional hexamer of spastin to lose the ability to execute the primary motion associated with the severing action. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Harmonization with MATCH enhances the generalizability of connectome-based machine learning predictors to new datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical data harmonization is necessary to mitigate site-related effects on structural connectomes while preserving biological information. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We prioritized and validated CVD-associated SNPs that created or destroyed genomic binding sites of the cardiac transcription factor GATA4. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results contribute to the mechanistic understanding of cardiovascular disease-associated non-coding variants impacting GATA4 function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DNA shape features ranked among the most predictive across all cancers. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CanDrivR-CS is designed to distinguish between rare and recurrent somatic missense variants in a cancer-specific context. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Error awareness is associated with significantly greater activity in a network of regions including the insula cortex, supramarginal gyrus, and anterior cingulate cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Error awareness is associated with significantly greater activity in a network of regions, including the insula cortex, supramarginal gyrus, and midline structures such as the anterior cingulate cortex and supplementary motor area. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Unconscious patients predicted to recover consciousness by ICU discharge had better long-term functional outcomes than those predicted to remain unconscious. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MRI lesions within key subcortical structures can help predict early recovery of consciousness and 3-month functional outcome. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Phenomic prediction (PP) had a higher correlation coefficient than genomic prediction (GP) for seed yield. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Genomic prediction (GP) had higher correlation coefficients for seed protein and oil contents compared to phenomic prediction (PP). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new GBLUP model and random forest model did not outperform the original GP and PP models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides insights on the inclusion of phenomic prediction (PP) and genomic prediction (GP) in soybean breeding programs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides insights on the inclusion of phenomic prediction (PP) and genomic prediction (GP) in soybean breeding programs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analyses indicated complex mutational patterns within and across viral proteins. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analyses indicated complex mutational patterns within and across viral proteins. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Specific protein regions and amino acid positions of several internal gene segments were more important for interspecies transmission. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed colocalization proxy approach accurately measures the nanoMOFs' colocalization from early to late endosomes and lysosomes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This preprint result maps directly to the publication's statement about accurate measurement of colocalization across endosomal compartments with essentially identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding the intracellular dynamics of nanoMOFs based on single-particle tracking is important for optimal and safe drug delivery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results emphasize the importance of understanding the intracellular dynamics of nanoMOFs based on single-particle tracking for optimal and safe drug delivery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both texts state the same concluding importance of single-particle tracking for safe and effective drug delivery with no meaningful change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: StarD4 facilitates non-vesicular sterol transport between the plasma membrane and the endoplasmic reticulum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: StarD4 protein facilitates non-vesicular cholesterol transport between the plasma membrane and the endoplasmic reticulum. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same facilitation claim, using 'cholesterol' instead of the more general 'sterol', but the meaning and scope remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The kinetics of sterol transfer by StarD4 is modulated by specific phosphatidylinositol phosphates (PIPs) on the target membrane. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recognition of PIP2 subtypes by StarD4 affects the direction and kinetics of cholesterol transport. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies PIP2 subtypes and frames the effect at medium certainty; this narrows and tempers the preprint's more general PIP/kinetics claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recognition of PIP2 subtypes by StarD4 impacts the direction and kinetics of cholesterol transport. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recognition of PIP2 subtypes by StarD4 affects the direction and kinetics of cholesterol transport. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This preprint conclusion maps directly to the publication's statement with the same specificity (PIP2 subtypes) and similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Function-related allosteric dynamics of StarD4 connect PIP2-subtype-specific conformational states to cholesterol binding modes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The specific molecular mechanism for recognizing PIP2-subtypes by StarD4 couples to an allosteric pathway that induces the cholesterol binding pocket to signal for either uptake or release of sterol. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both describe a specific allosteric mechanism coupling PIP2-subtype recognition to sterol/cholesterol binding behavior; the publication restates the mechanistic result with similar emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PIP2 subtypes play a crucial role in shaping functional StarD4 motifs responsible for organelle selectivity in cholesterol trafficking. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An allosteric mechanism connects the sterol binding site to the membrane-embedded part of StarD4, allowing it to respond differently to various organelle membranes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the functional consequence as an allosteric mechanism that enables organelle-specific responses; this clarifies the preprint's conclusion about organelle selectivity driven by PIP2 subtypes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: cyCONDOR is an easy-to-use computational framework that covers all essential steps of cytometry data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: cyCONDOR is an easy-to-use computational framework that covers all essential steps of cytometry data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The comprehensive suite of features of cyCONDOR facilitates its integration into clinically relevant settings. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comprehensive suite of features of cyCONDOR facilitates the seamless integration of HDC into clinically relevant settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the domain (HDC) and emphasizes seamless integration; this is a clarification of the preprint's general integration claim without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: cyCONDOR provides advanced analytical features such as pseudotime analysis and batch integration to extract deeper insights from data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: cyCONDOR provides advanced analytical features such as pseudotime analysis and batch integration to extract deeper insights from data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the result claim about advanced analytical features exactly, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: cyCONDOR has demonstrated versatility in assisting the analysis of high dimensionality data from preprocessing to biological interpretation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: cyCONDOR is an easy-to-use computational framework that covers all essential steps of cytometry data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim about covering essential steps overlaps with the preprint's statement of versatility across the analysis pipeline; the publication frames this as a core contribution, clarifying the preprint result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The current analytical platforms for HDC data analysis are either limited in scalability or designed for expert computational biologists. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation statement about existing platforms is present in the publication but was not asserted in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct biofilm formation categories were identified among 385 non-aureus Staphylococcus isolates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different routes to biofilm formation have been identified in diverse species of Staphylococcus. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some protein domains previously linked to biofilm formation were confirmed, while others not previously associated were also identified. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning identified amino acid 20-mers linked to biofilm formation, including important domains not previously associated with biofilm formation in staphylococci. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe identification of previously unassociated domains relevant to biofilm formation; the publication specifies the machine-learning-derived 20-mers but preserves the preprint's meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prevalence and distribution of biofilm associated domains are linked to phylogeny, indicating independent evolution of biofilm production mechanisms in different Staphylococcus species. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prevalence and distribution of biofilm associated domains are linked to phylogeny, indicating independent evolution of biofilm production mechanisms in different Staphylococcus species. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this phylogeny-linked conclusion verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding the mechanisms of biofilm formation in any given species requires detailed study of relevant strains, limiting the ability to generalize across the genus. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit limitation about the need for strain-level study limiting generalization; this preprint limitation does not appear in the listed publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: RT-qPCR confirmed the expression of selected genes predicted to encode important domains within biofilms in Staphylococcus epidermidis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This experimental RT-qPCR confirmation appears as a new published result not present among the preprint claims, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Negative self-schema has the largest causal effect on psychosis proneness with an effect size of 0.55. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Negative self-schema has the largest potential causal effect on psychosis proneness (PPS). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Social cohesion and trust have a protective causal effect on psychosis proneness with an effect size of -0.18. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low levels of social cohesion and trust have a potential causal effect on PPS. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Psychosis proneness is a direct cause of greater activation in the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex during working memory manipulation with an effect size of 0.14. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PPS is a direct cause of greater activation in the dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (DLPFC). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Causal discovery analysis provides directionality among the 37 variables related to psychosis proneness. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight the significance of negative self-schema and social cohesion and trust in the general population with psychosis proneness. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the substantive findings and their significance (higher certainty), which derives from the preprint's causal-discovery method; this represents a shift toward highlighting implications rather than the methodological statement itself.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study is the first to use data-driven analysis to model causal mechanisms in psychosis proneness in the general population. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study represents the first data-driven analysis to model causal mechanisms in psychosis proneness in the general population. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both the preprint and publication make the same contribution claim about being the first data-driven causal analysis in the general population.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods generally excel for closely related strains in predicting antimicrobial resistance phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning methods generally excel for closely related strains in predicting antimicrobial resistance phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ResFinder outperforms machine learning methods when handling divergent genomes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kover ranked top among the machine learning approaches evaluated in this study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Kover ranked top among the machine learning approaches evaluated in this study. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of antimicrobial resistance phenotype predictions varies substantially across species-antibiotic combinations, particularly for beta-lactams. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Campylobacter jejuni and Enterococcus faecium phenotypes are more robustly predicted than those of several other tested organisms. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neuroimaging-based pain biomarkers, when combined with machine learning techniques, have demonstrated potential in decoding pain intensity and diagnosing clinical pain conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings revealed a preference for population-level modeling with brain-wide features, aligning with the goal of clinical translation of neuroimaging biomarkers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Data levels, spatial scales, and sample sizes significantly impact model performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating more pain-related brain regions, increasing sample sizes, and averaging less data during training and more data during testing improved performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: High-ranking anomalous nodes identified by WGAND are enriched for proteins associated with tissue-specific diseases and biological processes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vilazodone (VLZ) promotes megakaryocyte differentiation and thrombopoiesis in cells and zebrafish models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Scm 6 A is a novel, dependable, and accurate method for single-cell m 6 A detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Altered thalamo-hippocampal RsFC is a potential new biomarker for TLE treatment resistance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using only the dynamic and transient phase of gesture movements significantly enhances the temporal resolution of human-robot interfaces. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using only the dynamic and transient phase of gesture movements significantly enhances the temporal resolution of human-robot interfaces. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach demonstrates state-of-the-art performance for gesture prediction in terms of accuracy, training time, and model convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach demonstrates state-of-the-art performance for gesture prediction in terms of accuracy, training time, and model convergence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of a high number of gestures (e.g., 65) adds to the complexity and significance of the motor intention decoding problem. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inclusion of a high number of gestures (e.g., 65) adds to the complexity and significance of the motor intention decoding problem. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive point about gesture set size and complexity remains; the publication frames it as a conclusion rather than a contribution but the content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The input-output function of the network can be exactly mapped to that of a deep linear network in the thermodynamic limit. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include any claim about an exact mapping to a deep linear network in the thermodynamic limit, so this preprint claim appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modifications of synaptic weights in lower levels of the hierarchy are both sufficient and necessary for perceptual learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modifying synaptic weights in the first layer of the hierarchy is both sufficient and necessary for perceptual learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates necessity and sufficiency but specifies the first layer rather than 'lower levels', narrowing and clarifying the original claim without changing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perceptual learning dynamics are constrained by a normative 'minimum perturbation' principle, favoring weight matrices with minimal changes relative to their pre-learning values. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perceptual learning dynamics are constrained by a normative minimum perturbation principle, which favors weight matrices with minimal changes relative to their prelearning values. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same normative minimum-perturbation principle with essentially identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Minimum perturbation plasticity induces changes to weights and neural representations in all layers of the network, except for the readout weight vector. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weight changes in higher layers of the network are not necessary for learning but help reduce overall perturbation to the network. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication weakens the universality of layer-wise changes: instead of changes in all layers (except readout), it reports higher-layer changes are not necessary, which revises and narrows the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The statistical mechanics theory of perceptual learning provides mechanistic and normative understanding of several important empirical findings. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The statistical mechanics theory of perceptual learning provides mechanistic and normative understanding of several important empirical findings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this contribution/claim verbatim in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Objects’ real-world size is encoded by an independent component in deep convolutional neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) encode objects’ size as an independent axis of the object space, similar to the human brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the independent encoding claim for DCNNs but explicitly adds a comparison to the human brain, broadening the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The removal of the component encoding real-world size significantly impaired the performance of DCNNs in recognizing objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report an explicit lesion/removal experiment or its effects on recognition performance, so this specific intervention result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shape, rather than texture, accounts for the representation of real-world size in deep convolutional networks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The shape of objects is critical to inferring objects’ size for both DCNNs and humans, rather than retinal size, context, task demands, or texture features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim (higher certainty) and extends it to humans and additional alternative explanations, amplifying and broadening the original point.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A follow-up fMRI experiment demonstrated that humans use shape, but not texture, to infer the real-world size of objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The shape of objects is critical to inferring objects’ size for both DCNNs and humans, rather than retinal size, context, task demands, or texture features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication incorporates the human result into a broader claim about shape being critical for both DCNNs and humans, keeping the core finding while widening context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides the first evidence supporting the feature of objects’ real-world size as an axis of object space. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep convolutional neural networks (DCNNs) encode objects’ size as an independent axis of the object space, similar to the human brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the axis-of-object-space conclusion and additionally frames it in relation to the human brain, broadening the original concluding claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study devises a paradigm that supplements conventional approaches to explore the structure of object space. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a methodological/contribution statement about a new paradigm that does not appear as an explicit claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibition of the network bursting activity in response to RF exposure became apparent at an SAR level of 28.6 W/kg. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inhibition of the network bursting activity in response to RF exposure became apparent at an SAR level of 28.6 W/kg. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this numerical/experimental result directly and without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Exposure to RF fields preferentially inhibits bursting over spiking activity and exerts fewer constraints on neural network bursting synchrony. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Exposure to RF fields preferentially inhibits bursting over spiking activity and exerts fewer constraints on neural network bursting synchrony. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparative inhibition finding verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Network rebound excitation was observed following the removal of tonic hyperpolarization after washout of MU but not in response to cessation of RF exposure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim set does not report this specific rebound observation, so this particular preprint result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The narrowing effect on action potential seen under RF exposure might contribute to reducing network bursting activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The narrowing effect on action potential seen under RF exposure might contribute to reducing network bursting activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this mechanistic suggestion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inhibitory mechanisms of the action of RF fields differ from the ones mediated by the activation of GABA A receptors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inhibitory mechanisms of the action of RF fields differ from the ones mediated by the activation of GABAA receptors. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion but states it with higher confidence (amplified certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Healthcare guided by semantic segmentation has the potential to improve our quality of life through early and accurate disease detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Healthcare guided by semantic segmentation has the potential to improve our quality of life through early and accurate disease detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level conclusion identically.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Chimeric U-Net, a U-Net architecture with an invertible decoder unit, inherently brings explainability into semantic segmentation tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this architectural contribution as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The invertible decoder helps to disentangle the class information in the latent space embedding and to construct meaningful saliency maps. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The invertible decoder helps to disentangle the class information in the latent space embedding and to construct meaningful saliency maps. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The latent space, constructed by the Chimeric U-Net, encodes an interpretable representation of the segmentation quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The latent space, constructed by the Chimeric U-Net, encodes an interpretable representation of the segmentation quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this empirical result as stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed constraints on the architecture induce explainability, revealing class correlations and local contextual dependencies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed constraints on the architecture induce explainability, revealing class correlations and local contextual dependencies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this concluding claim about architectural constraints and explainability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adaptation acts proactively by optimally adjusting sensory encoding for future sensory input. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Adaptation leads to a reallocation of coding resources such that encoding accuracy peaks at the mean orientation of the adaptor while total coding capacity remains constant. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distribution of local visual orientations in the retinal input stream is peaked at the mean orientation computed over a short input history. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A recurrent neural network trained to predict the next frame of natural scene videos exhibited the same change in encoding accuracy as observed in human subjects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network exhibited the same change in encoding accuracy as observed in human subjects when simulating the human adaptation experiment. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network model learns place-like spatial representations and exhibits remapping abilities similar to biological place cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network model demonstrates that output units learn place-like spatial representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim that units learn place-like representations but separates/removes the remapping detail into a distinct claim, thus clarifying and narrowing the original combined statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's place unit centers form clusters organized in a hexagonal lattice in open fields.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary evidence suggests that the center locations of CA1 place fields in mice exhibit a clustering tendency similar to the hexagonal lattice organization observed in the model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Preliminary evidence suggests a clustering tendency of CA1 place fields in mice, indicating a potential mechanism for the interaction between place cells, border cells, and grid cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary evidence suggests that the center locations of CA1 place fields in mice exhibit a clustering tendency similar to the hexagonal lattice organization observed in the model.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the mouse CA1 clustering more directly and explicitly links it to the model observation, raising the stated confidence from preliminary/low to a medium-level result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed non-trainable decoding scheme is inspired by the localized firing patterns of place cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provides a normative framework for learning spatial representations that were previously reserved for biological place cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution from a specific non-trainable decoding scheme to a broader normative framework for learning spatial representations, expanding scope and increasing asserted confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel interaction was identified between the first lumenal loop of PTCHD1 and the SNARE-associated protein SNAPIN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel interaction was identified between the first lumenal loop of PTCHD1 and the SNARE-associated protein SNAPIN.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Clinically associated missense variants within this region did not disrupt SNAPIN binding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Six of the 12 missense variants tested exhibited pronounced retention within the endoplasmic reticulum, and impaired neuronal and non-neuronal trafficking to the plasma membrane.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Six of the twelve missense variants tested exhibited pronounced retention within the endoplasmic reticulum and impaired neuronal and non-neuronal trafficking to the plasma membrane.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings provide a platform for diagnostic assay and VUS interpretation, allowing for clinical re-classification of these variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings provide a platform for diagnostic assay and VUS interpretation, allowing for clinical reclassification of these variants.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We establish a novel incompressible orthotropic constitutive neural network to simultaneously discover both model and parameters that best explain human cardiac tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We establish a novel incompressible orthotropic constitutive neural network to simultaneously discover both model and parameters that best explain human cardiac tissue.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate that we can successfully train the network with triaxial shear and biaxial extension tests and systematically sparsify the parameter vector with L1-regularization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate that we can successfully train the network with triaxial shear and biaxial extension tests.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We robustly discover a four-term model that features a quadratic term in the second invariant I2, and exponential quadratic terms in the fourth and eighth invariants I4f, I4n, and I8fs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We robustly discover a four-term model that features a quadratic term in the second invariant I2, and exponential quadratic terms in the fourth and eighth invariants I4f, I4n, and I8fs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our discovered model outperforms popular existing myocardium models and generalizes well from homogeneous laboratory tests to heterogeneous whole heart simulations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our discovered model outperforms popular existing myocardium models and generalizes well from homogeneous laboratory tests to heterogeneous whole heart simulations.
alignments.rationale: Performance and generalization claims are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automating the process of model discovery has the potential to democratize cardiac modeling, broaden participation in scientific discovery, and accelerate the development of innovative treatments for cardiovascular disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automating the process of model discovery has the potential to democratize cardiac modeling, broaden participation in scientific discovery, and accelerate the development of innovative treatments for cardiovascular disease.
alignments.rationale: The concluding broader-impact statement is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The direction selectivity of V1 simple cells emerges through the interplay between tuned intercortical inhibition and a temporal offset in the feedforward path through lagged LGN cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The direction selectivity of simple cells emerges through the interplay between tuned intercortical inhibition and a temporal offset in the feedforward path through lagged LGN cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this mechanistic result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Direction-selective simple cells are linked to a particular spiking pattern in a local excitatory-inhibitory circuit.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Intercortical inhibition alone leads to bidirectional selectivity.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the role of intercortical inhibition specifically (bidirectional selectivity), which clarifies the original statement about circuit spiking patterns into a more specific inhibitory-driven effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibitory neurons with a different spatial position or tuning prevent a simple cell from spiking when the stimulus moves in the non-preferred direction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Intercortical inhibition alone leads to bidirectional selectivity.
alignments.rationale: This inhibitory-prevention mechanism maps closely to the publication's claim that intercortical inhibition produces selectivity by suppressing responses in non-preferred directions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the preferred direction, inhibitory cells spike later, enabling the simple cell to spike.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A temporal shift in the thalamocortical pathway breaks the symmetry in favor of one direction, leading to unidirectional selectivity.
alignments.rationale: The publication attributes direction asymmetry to a temporal shift in the feedforward thalamocortical pathway rather than specifically to delayed inhibitory cell spiking, altering the mechanistic attribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings disclose an explicit spatial frequency coding at single-neuron and population levels in the inferior temporal cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explicit spatial frequency (SF) coding is present at both single-neuron and population levels in the inferior temporal cortex of macaques.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the finding directly with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Low spatial frequency is decoded faster than high spatial frequency, and the spatial frequency preference dynamically shifts from low to high over time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low SF (LSF) is decoded faster than high SF (HSF), and the SF preference shifts from LSF to HSF over time.
alignments.rationale: The temporal shift and decoding speed claim are preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatial frequency representation for each neuron forms a profile that predicts category selectivity at the population level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SF representation for each neuron forms a profile that predicts category selectivity at the population level.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries this neuron-level predictive-profile claim forward with equivalent wording and implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High spatial frequency-preferred neurons demonstrate the highest category decoding performance for face stimuli.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High SF-preferring neurons exhibit the highest category decoding for faces.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results dissociate the separate representations of spatial frequency and object category, underscoring the pivotal role of spatial frequency in object recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spatial frequency and category representations are uncorrelated, with SF being more sparsely represented and dependent on individual neurons.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the dissociation more precisely (uncorrelated representations and sparse SF coding) but preserves the original conclusion about separable representations and SF importance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The repertoire of hunting movements of cichlids is broader than that of zebrafish, but shares basic features such as eye convergence and discrete prey capture bouts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The repertoire of hunting movements of cichlids is broader than that of zebrafish, but shares basic features such as eye convergence and discrete prey capture bouts. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Medaka swim continuously and track prey monocularly without eye convergence, positioning prey laterally before capturing them with a side swing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Medaka swim continuously and track prey monocularly without eye convergence, positioning prey laterally before capturing them with a side swing. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Cichlids and zebrafish may mainly use binocular visual cues to judge distance to prey, while medaka may rely predominantly on motion parallax. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study documents the diversification of locomotor and oculomotor adaptations among hunting teleost larvae. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study documents the diversification of locomotor and oculomotor adaptations among hunting teleost larvae. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individual differences in face perception can largely be explained by variability in general object perception abilities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual differences in face perception can to a large part be explained by variability in general object perception abilities. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Additional variability in face processing can be attributed to visual similarity with faces as captured by dimensions of object space. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Additional variability in face processing can be attributed to visual similarity with faces as captured by dimensions of object space. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: People who struggle with telling apart faces also have difficulties with discriminating other objects with the same object space attributes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: People who struggle with telling apart faces also have difficulties with discriminating other objects with the same object space attributes. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is consistent with a contribution of object space to human visual cognition. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Half of MCH cells expressed CART and were most prominent in the medial hypothalamus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Half of MCH cells expressed CART and were most prominent in the medial hypothalamus. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Whole-cell patch-clamp recordings revealed differences in the passive and active membrane properties of MCH cells in a sex-dependent manner. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinctions in topographical division and cellular properties between MCH cells add to their heterogeneity and help elucidate their response to stimuli. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinctions in topographical division and cellular properties between MCH cells add to their heterogeneity and help elucidate their response to stimuli. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of distributed representations in sensory modalities is referred to as the assignment problem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation of one or more common stimulus features in pairs of relevant brain regions solves the assignment problem for integrating auditory and visual information. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the definitional claim to specify a solution mechanism and focuses on audiovisual integration, narrowing and clarifying the original general statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reliability of the solution to the assignment problem depends on features of the stimulus set and the details of the split representations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reliability of the solution to the assignment problem depends on features of the stimulus set and the details of the split representations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same dependency claim but reports it with reduced expressed certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When many spikes and neurons are available, representing stimuli from a single sensory modality can be done more reliably across multiple brain regions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A biologically plausible receptive field code demonstrates a tradeoff between local and catastrophic errors when representing stimuli across multiple brain regions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A feedforward neural network can learn the optimal solution to the assignment problem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A feedforward neural network can learn the optimal solution to the assignment problem with inputs in two distinct representational formats. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several key predictions of the theory regarding assignment errors have support from the human working memory literature. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Single units of multiple Aβ subtypes, especially slowly adapting type II (SA-II) and fast adapting hair follicle afferents (HFA), can reliably differentiate the skin contact of social touch expressions at accuracies similar to those perceptually. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Single units of multiple mechanoreceptive Aβ subtypes, especially slowly adapting type II (SA-II) and fast adapting hair follicle afferents (HFA), can reliably differentiate social touch expressions at accuracies similar to human recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rapidly adapting field (Field) afferents exhibit lower accuracies in differentiating social touch expressions, while C-tactile (CT), fast adapting Pacinian corpuscles (FA-II), and muscle spindle (MS) afferents can barely differentiate the expressions despite responding to the stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present a corresponding claim about the poor discriminative performance of Field, CT, FA-II, and MS afferents; that specific comparative result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most informative firing patterns of SA-II and HFA afferents’ spike trains indicate that an average duration of 3-4 s of firing provides sufficient discriminative information. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most informative firing patterns of SA-II and HFA afferents indicate that average durations of 3-4 s of firing provide sufficient discriminative information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core temporal-duration finding is identical, but the publication reports it with stronger expressed certainty (amplified).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SA-II and HFA afferents differentiate the skin contact of social touch at time scales relevant for such interactions, which is 1-2 orders of magnitude longer than those relevant for discriminating non-social touch inputs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SA-II and HFA afferents differentiate the skin contact of social touch at time scales relevant for such interactions, which are 1-2 orders of magnitude longer than those for non-social touch. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about longer timescales for social versus non-social touch is preserved verbatim and with the same certainty.
