alignments.rationale: The publication adjusts the characterization (novel -> nonlinear) and generalizes the ecological function from horizon transitions to broader 'changes in visual context', altering both phrasing and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We confirmed experimentally that transient Suppressed-by-Contrast RGCs (tSbC) responded very selectively to Green-OFF, UV-ON contrasts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We confirmed experimentally that transient suppressed-by-contrast (tSbC) RGCs responded very selectively to Green-OFF, UV-ON contrasts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental confirmation of the specific chromatic selectivity is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: tSbCs reliably detected transitions from ground to sky in the visual scene, as might be elicited by head- or eye-movements across the horizon. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: tSbC cells performed best among all RGC types at reliably detecting transitions from ground to sky in the visual scene. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a comparative performance statement ('performed best among all RGC types'), clarifying and strengthening the original conclusion about reliable detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We suggest a role for tSbCs in providing contextual information necessary for the selection of appropriate behavioural responses to other stimuli. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We suggest a role for tSbC RGCs in providing contextual information necessary for the selection of appropriate behavioural responses to other stimuli. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The speculative functional suggestion about tSbC cells is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work showcases how a combination of experiments with natural stimuli and computational modelling allows discovering novel types of stimulus selectivity. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state this methodological/conceptual contribution about combining natural stimuli and modeling, so the preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants were able to use implicit information to improve performance despite unawareness of the cue-target contingencies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants were able to use implicit information to improve performance despite unawareness of the cue-target contingencies.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are effectively identical in wording and meaning; no change in scope or confidence is evident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Implicit inferences influenced responses to subsequent targets despite a lack of awareness of cue-target contingencies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Implicit inferences influenced responses to subsequent targets despite a lack of awareness of cue-target contingencies.
alignments.rationale: The claim is repeated verbatim between preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Implicit inferences emerge through interactions between internally- and externally-oriented neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Implicit inferences emerge through interactions between internally and externally oriented neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanistic claim with only minor stylistic rephrasing; meaning and scope are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The anterior prefrontal cortex plays an important role in transforming external events into predictive internalized models of the world.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prefrontal processes are important in transforming external events into predictive internalized models of the world.
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the locus from anterior prefrontal cortex to prefrontal processes generally and lowers certainty (high->medium), indicating a revision and tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended generalized leaky integrate-and-fire (E-GLIF) neuron model can generate complex electroresponsive properties of brain neurons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extended generalized leaky integrate-and-fire (E-GLIF) neuron model can generate complex electroresponsive properties of brain neurons.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E-GLIF was validated against experimental electrophysiological data recorded from cerebellar Golgi cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: E-GLIF was validated against experimental electrophysiological data recorded from cerebellar Golgi cells.
alignments.rationale: Validation claim carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E-GLIF can reproduce complex neuronal dynamics, including intensity-frequency curves and spontaneous subthreshold oscillations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: E-GLIF can reproduce complex neuronal dynamics, including intensity-frequency curves and spontaneous subthreshold oscillations.
alignments.rationale: Same experimental/result claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: E-GLIF provides a new effective tool to investigate brain dynamics in large-scale simulations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: E-GLIF provides a new effective tool to investigate brain dynamics in large-scale simulations.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion carried over without substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cardiac myosin binding protein C (mybpc3) regulates shortening velocity by transient N-terminus binding to actin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cardiac myosin binding protein C (mybpc3) regulates shortening velocity by transient N-terminus binding to actin.
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic result is repeated verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonsynonymous single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) cause single residue substitutions that influence phenotype and pathogenicity characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonsynonymous single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) cause single residue substitutions that influence phenotype and pathogenicity characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about SNP effects is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evident subpopulation bias in myosin SNP pathogenicities implies that myosin normally engages other sarcomere proteins functionally.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evident subpopulation bias in myosin SNP pathogenicities implies that myosin engages multiple sarcomere proteins functionally.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly rephrases the implication ("other" -> "multiple") and removes 'normally', a minor wording clarification without changing the substantive inference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The working models and the integral myosin/mybpc3 motor concept suggest a novel strain-dependent mechanism adapting myosin force-velocity to load dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The working models and the integral myosin/mybpc3 motor concept suggest a novel strain-dependent mechanism adapting myosin force-velocity to load dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion is preserved intact between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 31% of the children with neurodevelopmental disorders had AOR and 27% had TOR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 31% of the children with neurodevelopmental disorders had auditory over-responsivity (AOR) and 27% had tactile over-responsivity (TOR).
alignments.rationale: The prevalence results are the same; publication expands abbreviations but preserves content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Children with AOR had three white matter tracts showing decreased fractional anisotropy relative to children without AOR.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Children with AOR had three white matter tracts showing decreased fractional anisotropy relative to children without AOR.
alignments.rationale: The neuroimaging finding is reproduced without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SSP parent report and SP-3D:A direct observation ratings overlapped moderately for sensory related behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inter-test agreement between the Short Sensory Profile (SSP) and Sensory Processing-Three Dimensions: Assessment (SP-3D:A) for AOR was 65% and for TOR was 50%.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives precise numeric agreement values, clarifying the earlier qualitative statement of moderate overlap.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study identified cut scores for AOR and TOR using the SSP parent report and SP-3D:A observation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study identified cut-off scores for AOR and TOR using the SSP parent report and SP-3D:A observation.
alignments.rationale: The identification of cut scores/cut-offs is the same claim with only wording variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A combination of questionnaire and direct observation measures should be used in clinical and research settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A combination of questionnaire and direct observation measures should be used in clinical and research settings.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is carried forward identically into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rapid changes in multivariate activity patterns are observable with fMRI during periods of rapid behavioral change.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the fMRI-observability claim about rapid behavioral change; no clear matching publication claim was found.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multivariate patterns of activity in several cortical regions contain representations that change more rapidly during periods of uncertainty following a change in behavioral context.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multivariate patterns of activity in several cortical regions change more rapidly during periods of uncertainty following a change in behavioral context.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core finding about faster-changing multivariate representations during uncertainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In motor cortex, rapid changes in activity patterns are indicative of discontinuous change in behavioral outputs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In motor cortex, rapid changes in activity patterns are indicative of discontinuous change in behavioral outputs.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about motor cortex pattern changes and behavioral discontinuities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In visual regions, rapid changes in activity patterns are evoked by tracking salient environmental changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In visual regions, rapid changes in activity patterns are evoked by tracking salient environmental changes.
alignments.rationale: The claim about visual-region responses to salient changes is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Representations in orbitofrontal cortex evolve more rapidly during periods of rapid learning, providing a dynamic substrate for learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In orbitofrontal cortex, rapid changes in activity patterns are best explained by representations of a shifting context that evolve more rapidly during periods of rapid learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication elaborates the mechanism (shifting context representations) underlying the OFC finding, clarifying rather than changing the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Errors and noise during learning increase the probability of memory recall. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Errors and noise during learning increase the probability of memory recall. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a tradeoff between the capacity and reliability of stored memories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a trade-off between the capacity and reliability of stored memories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same trade-off claim with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Noise during learning is required for optimal retrieval of stored information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Noise during learning is required for optimal retrieval of stored information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result unchanged in content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Networks loaded with associative memories to capacity display many structural and dynamical features observed in local cortical circuits. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Networks loaded with associative memories to capacity display many structural and dynamical features observed in local cortical circuits in mammals. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'in mammals' and reclassifies contribution type, clarifying the biological context without changing the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article predicts that connections from unreliable inhibitory and excitatory neurons must be depressed or eliminated during learning. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Connections originating from more unreliable neurons in the cortex are more likely to be depressed or eliminated during learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the prediction to cortical 'more unreliable neurons' (narrowing cell-type specificity) and reports it with higher certainty, strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Older adults exhibit greater multisensory response time facilitation by violating the race model compared to younger adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Older adults exhibit greater multisensory response time facilitation by violating the race model compared to young adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about greater race-model violation in older adults with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Older adults have wider temporal binding windows and points of subjective simultaneity that are farther from true simultaneity than younger adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Older adults typically exhibit wider temporal binding windows and points of subjective simultaneity that lie farther from true simultaneity compared to young adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this age-related temporal binding result verbatim in meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant positive relation between the magnitude of race model violation in younger adults and the points of subjective simultaneity obtained from the audiovisual temporal order judgment task, which is absent among the elderly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significant positive relation between the magnitude of race model violation in young adults and the points of subjective simultaneity obtained from the audiovisual temporal-order judgment task, which is absent in older adults. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same correlation pattern across age groups with equivalent certainty and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Audiovisual temporal processing changes with age. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct processes are likely involved in simultaneity and temporal-order perception. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes distinct perceptual processes rather than the broad statement that audiovisual temporal processing changes with age; this represents a substantive conceptual shift and lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Common processing between race model violation and temporal order judgment is impaired in the elderly. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Common processing between race model violation and temporal-order judgment is impaired in the elderly. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but reports it with downgraded certainty (high -> medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensory neocortex incorporates movement using grid cell-like neurons that represent the location of sensors on an object. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sensory neocortex incorporates movement using grid cell-like neurons that represent the location of sensors on an object. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same core conclusion about grid cell-like representations with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A two-layer neural network model can robustly learn and recognize objects through movement and predict sensory stimuli after movement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A two-layer neural network model can robustly learn and recognize objects through movement and predict sensory stimuli after movement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this modeling result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can learn hundreds of objects even when object features alone are insufficient for disambiguation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can learn hundreds of objects even when object features alone are insufficient for disambiguation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this quantitative modeling claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reciprocal connections between layers 4 and 6 of the cortical circuitry fit the requirements of the proposed model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reciprocal connections between layers 4 and 6 of the cortical circuitry fit the requirements of the proposed model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same anatomical-conclusion mapping without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The subgranular layers of cortical columns employ grid cell-like mechanisms to represent object specific locations that are updated through movement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The subgranular layers of cortical columns employ grid cell-like mechanisms to represent object specific locations that are updated through movement. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this mechanistic conclusion as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Connectivity between and within functional neural networks changes from childhood to adulthood. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Age-related increasing segregation between the precuneus and LFPN at rest contributes to the enhanced distinction of processing states in the precuneus. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies a particular mechanism (precuneus-LFPN segregation at rest) explaining developmental connectivity changes, narrowing the original broad developmental claim and reporting it with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Engaging in a task results in greater precuneus-LFPN and precuneus-RFPN connectivity compared to rest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Systematic state-dependent functional connectivity in the precuneus is observed, with greater connectivity to LFPN and RFPN during task engagement compared to rest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same state-dependent connectivity result with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The degree to which the precuneus distinguishes between task and rest states increases with age. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The degree to which the precuneus distinguishes between task and rest states increases with age. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this developmental result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distinct role of the precuneus in tracking processing state is both present throughout and strengthened across development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The distinct role of the precuneus in tracking processing state is both present throughout development and strengthened across age. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this developmental-conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) integrates the current linguistic environment with everyday linguistic experience during language comprehension. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) integrates the current linguistic environment with everyday linguistic experience, showing higher activation when the input does not match everyday experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the ACC role by explicitly linking integration to higher activation when input deviates from everyday experience while preserving the core result and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unexpectedly frequent syntactic structures lead to higher activation in the ACC compared to other conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The anterior cingulate cortex (ACC) integrates the current linguistic environment with everyday linguistic experience, showing higher activation when the input does not match everyday experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the higher ACC activation in terms of mismatch with everyday experience, which clarifies the preprint's observation about unexpectedly frequent structures without changing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased activation occurs in the language network when there is a mismatch between verb preference and syntactic structure presentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Verb-based surprisal leads to increased activation in the language network and the precuneus when there is a mismatch between verb preference and structure presentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds involvement of the precuneus to the preprint's language-network activation, broadening the neural scope while keeping the core finding and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Brain areas engaged in cognitive control, such as the ACC, might use statistics of syntactic structures to facilitate language comprehension. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Brain areas engaged in prediction and error signaling, such as the ACC, use the statistics of syntactic structures to modulate language processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases 'cognitive control' as 'prediction and error signaling' but preserves the conclusion that such areas use syntactic statistics to influence processing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The language network is directly engaged in processing verb preferences, indicating distinct influences on sentence processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The language network is directly engaged in processing verb preferences, indicating distinct influences on sentence processing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion verbatim in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human observers were poorer at tracking objects between versus within hemifields, reflecting a cost of interhemispheric integration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human observers were poorer at tracking objects between versus within hemifields, reflecting a cost of interhemispheric integration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gamma coherence was greater in trials requiring interhemispheric integration, particularly between sensors over parieto-occipital areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gamma coherence was greater in trials requiring interhemispheric integration, particularly between sensors over parieto-occipital areas. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with identical content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The observed cost of integration was associated with a failure of the cerebral hemispheres to become coherent in the gamma band in approximately half of the participants. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observed cost of integration was associated with a failure of the cerebral hemispheres to become coherent in the gamma band in approximately half of the participants. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same observation and level of certainty as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Instantaneous phase-coherence over the tracking period uniquely predicted between-hemifield tracking performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Instantaneous phase coherence over the tracking period uniquely predicted between-hemifield tracking performance, suggesting that effective integration benefits from sustained interhemispheric synchronization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the original predictive result but adds an interpretive sentence linking sustained synchronization to effective integration, clarifying and slightly amplifying the mechanistic inference.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gamma coherence mediates interhemispheric integration during multiple object tracking. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gamma coherence mediates interhemispheric integration during multiple object tracking and drives communication across cortically distributed neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the conclusion to assert an additional role for gamma coherence in driving communication across distributed networks, broadening the claim and strengthening mechanistic interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensively trained skills show vulnerability to interference from subsequent training of another skill. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensively trained skills show vulnerability to interference from subsequent training of another skill. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical finding without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The magnitude of interference depends on the relative amounts of training devoted to the different tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The magnitude of interference depends on the relative amounts of training devoted to the different tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pattern of interference observed in human participants closely matched the model predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pattern of interference observed in human participants closely matched the predictions made by the recurrent neural network model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies which model generated the predictions (recurrent neural network), making the claim more explicit without changing its substance or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Behavioral interference can be explained by antagonistic changes in neuronal tuning induced by the two tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Behavioral interference can be explained by antagonistic changes in neuronal tuning induced by the two tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic conclusion is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Approximate Bayesian Computations (ABC) framework developed can reconstruct complex admixture histories from independent genetic markers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Approximate Bayesian Computation (ABC) framework developed can reconstruct highly complex admixture histories from independent genetic markers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution claim but intensifies wording ('highly complex'), a minor clarification without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random-Forest ABC scenario-choice, followed by Neural-Network ABC posterior parameter estimation, can distinguish most complex admixture scenarios and provide accurate model-parameter estimations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random forest ABC scenario-choice can accurately distinguish among most complex admixture scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the original bundled claim into separate statements; this mapping aligns the preprint's scenario-choice portion with the RF claim while the neural-network parameter-estimation statement appears separately in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Monotonically decreasing contributions over time from the European and African sources explain the observed data more accurately than multiple admixture pulses for both admixed populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Monotonically decreasing contributions over time from Europe and Africa explained the observed data more accurately than multiple admixture pulses for the studied populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this empirical model-comparison result with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contrasted trajectories of introgression decay from the European and African sources are found between the two admixed populations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about contrasted introgression trajectories is not represented in the set of publication claims and appears omitted from the final paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MetHis software can simulate independent SNPs or microsatellites in a two-way admixed population for scenarios with multiple admixture pulses. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This software/contribution claim does not appear among the preprint claims and is presented newly in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network ABC posterior parameter estimation was accurate and reasonably conservative under complex admixture scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Although related to the preprint's combined-method claim, the publication separates and frames the neural-network parameter-estimation performance as its own statement not present verbatim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Kinetic, simplified models are better suited to model ionic channels compared to Hodgkin and Huxley models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simplified Markov-type kinetic models are more suitable for ion channel modeling compared to Hodgkin and Huxley models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same high-level conclusion, with terminology made explicit (Markov-type), but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Hodgkin and Huxley model has a conflicting practical limitation as it only supplies one parameter to model two distinct electrophysiological behaviours. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Hodgkin and Huxley model has a conflicting practical limitation as it only supplies one parameter to model two distinct electrophysiological behaviors. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The practical limitation is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A step by step procedure is provided to correctly optimize the kinetic model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A step by step optimization procedure for both Markov-type and Hodgkin and Huxley models can be rationally carried out. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the procedural claim to apply to both model classes and tones the stated certainty to medium while keeping the core contribution (an optimization procedure).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simplified kinetic models are currently the best option to closely approximate the known complexity of the ionic channel macroscopic currents. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simplified Markov-type kinetic models can closely approximate the known complexity of the macroscopic currents of ion channels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the strong 'best option' conclusion to a claim that Markov-type models 'can closely approximate' macroscopic currents, reducing the asserted superiority and reframing as a result rather than the strongest conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimization of kinetic models results in computational burdens comparable with those from Hodgkin and Huxley models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimization of Markov-type models allows for a computational burden comparable to that of Hodgkin and Huxley models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the comparison of computational burden and similar level of certainty, rephrased but substantively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Constitutive models for warp knitted fabrics are highly sensitive to an accurate representation of the textile microstructure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Constitutive models for warp knitted fabrics are highly sensitive to an accurate representation of the textile microstructure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical finding exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models with three microstructural directions outperform classical orthotropic models with only two in-plane directions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models with three microstructural directions outperform classical orthotropic models with only two in-plane directions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this comparative modeling result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We consistently discover models with two exponential linear fourth invariant terms that inherently capture the initial flexibility of the virgin mesh and the pronounced nonlinear stiffening as the loops of the mesh tighten. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific technical claim about the discovered functional form (two exponential linear fourth invariant terms) does not appear among the publication claims and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tools developed in this study will generalize naturally to other textile fabrics, enabling the robust discovery of anisotropic constitutive models for a wide variety of textile structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tools developed in this study will enable the robust discovery of anisotropic constitutive models for a wide variety of textile structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about the tools' ability to enable robust discovery but tightens wording (dropping 'generalize naturally'), a minor clarification without scope change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated model discovery is envisioned as a novel strategy for the generative material design of wearable devices, stretchable electronics, and smart fabrics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Dephosphorylation of pSer51 of the α subunit of translation initiation factor 2 (eIF2α<sup>P</sup>) terminates signaling in the integrated stress response (ISR).
