alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study suggests a future key role for LFP in the development of network models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The local field potential (LFP) can be used to validate and infer properties of mechanistic cortical network models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The amount of information transfer between two oscillating neural populations can be determined solely by their connection delay and the mismatch in their oscillation frequencies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The amount of information transfer between two oscillating neural populations is determined by their connection delay and the mismatch in their oscillation frequencies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The collective phase response curve of the oscillating neural populations is crucial for the efficacy of signal transmission and the quality of information transfer in brain networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The collective phase response curve of the oscillating neural populations plays a crucial role in the efficacy of signal transmission in brain networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The populations advancing in phase to others do not necessarily play the role of the information source. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The amount and direction of information transfer depend on the oscillation frequency of the populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The amount of information transfer between two oscillating neural populations is determined by their connection delay and the mismatch in their oscillation frequencies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple and generic coarse-grained model for DNA-protein and protein-protein interactions has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple and generic coarse-grained model for DNA-protein and protein-protein interactions has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology presented is broadly applicable to DNA-binding proteins and provides a systematic approach for analyzing mesoscale complexes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Model performance increased with increasing taxonomic resolution, up to the family level where performance was equal among family, genus, and OTU levels. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a trade-off between taxonomic resolution and prediction performance, where coarse taxonomic resolution is not distinct enough but fine resolution is too individualized to accurately classify samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a trade-off between taxonomic resolution and prediction performance, where coarse taxonomic resolution is not distinct enough, but fine resolution is too individualized to accurately classify samples. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Mid-range resolution (i.e., family, genus, and OTU) is optimal for prediction of colorectal cancer from microbiome data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Thousands of genes remain unaffected by increased AoXlnR expression despite having AoXlnR-binding motifs in their promoter region. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DNA structural information around the binding site of transcription factors can be used to predict the involvement of the binding site in the regulation of downstream gene expression. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DNA structural information around the binding site of transcription factors can be used to predict the involvement of the binding site in the regulation of downstream gene expression. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are site-specific biases that affect the frequency of insertions of the Himar1 transposon at different TA sites. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The non-permissive pattern (CG)GnTAnC(CG) suppresses insertions, while an A in the -3 position or T in the +3 position from the TA site encourages them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are site-specific biases that affect the frequency of insertion of the Himar1 transposon at different TA sites in Mycobacterium tuberculosis. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final predictive model explains about half of the variance in insertion counts, with the remainder attributed to stochastic variability or sampling differences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed quantitative model can explain up to half of the variance in insertion counts based on nucleotide context. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TTN-Fitness method improves the identification of conditionally essential genes or genetic interactions by using a site-specific model of insertion preferences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TTN-Fitness method can better distinguish true biological fitness effects compared to previous essentiality methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
